t_ch: 但，我明白我错了。能将「音符小姐」召唤现界，意味着圣杯确实拥有某种能力——而将自己朦胧未明的愿望托付给不可知的存在，就是一切危险的开始。
t_en: But now, I realize I was wrong. Summoning Miss Note to the present world showed me that the Holy Grail does possess some sort of power — and entrusting my vague, unclear wish to an unknown entity would lead us down a very dangerous path.

t_ch: 不管为了谁，我的愿望依然自私，它和我兄长的愿望并没有区别。很抱歉，音符小姐，在召唤出你的那一刻，我也明白了自己的心意。
t_en: Regardless of my intentions, my wish is still selfish. It's no different from my brother's wish. I'm sorry, Miss Note, but the moment I summoned you, I realized my true feelings.

t_ch: 匹诺康尼的人们没有脆弱到依靠「太一之梦」的拯救，我也自然不应该向圣杯祈求些什么。我更关心的是，老奥帝先生到底想利用圣杯做什么？
t_en: The people of Penacony are not so weak that they need "Ena's Dream" to save them. I shouldn't ask the Holy Grail for anything. What concerns me more is... what exactly does Old Oti want to use the Holy Grail for?

t_ch: 这场「圣杯战争」确实蹊跷。诸位也许不知道，在我的世界，圣杯战争原本是魔术师们的仪式，它需要保持仪式的神秘性，以获得创造奇迹的力量。
t_en: This Holy Grail War is indeed suspicious. You may not know, but in my world, the Holy Grail War was originally a ritual for mages, one that needed to preserve its mysticism in order to obtain the power to create miracles.

t_ch: 但这位老奥帝先生，作为举办人却大张旗鼓地将仪式公之于众？这本该让它受到玷污，无法进行。
t_en: But this Old Oti, the host, is openly displaying the ritual to the public? This should have tainted it, making it impossible to carry out.

t_ch: 我经历过很多次圣杯战争，也见识过各种不同的仪式形态。但这一次，和过去全都不同。
t_en: I've experienced many Holy Grail Wars and seen many different ritual forms, but this one is unlike anything before.

t_ch: 在我看来，慎重对待是上策。御主，你怎么看？
t_en: In my view, it's best to proceed with caution. Master, what do you think?

t_ch: 统统打倒就好！
t_en: Let's just beat them all!

t_ch: 不是啦…是让大家团结起来，暂时不要争斗。
t_en: No, no... We must unite everyone, temporarily setting aside any fighting.

t_ch: 向他们求饶！
t_en: We should surrender and beg for mercy!

t_ch: 是求和…不，是谈和。至少要向他们传达我们无意争斗的想法。
t_en: It's about making peace... not begging. At the very least, we should show them we have no intention of fighting.

t_ch: 让大家团结起来！
t_en: Let's unite everyone!

t_ch: 没错，就是这样。
t_en: That's right, exactly.

t_ch: 以我对奥帝的了解，他无论如何都不会停止这场游戏。
t_en: From what I know of Old Oti, he won't stop this game no matter what.

t_ch: 所以，我希望结成一个参战者的同盟，人数尽可能多。在明确这场圣杯战争的真正意图，以及圣杯的力量本质前，不要轻易许愿。
t_en: So, I want to form an alliance with as many participants as possible. Until we clearly understand the true nature of this Holy Grail War and the power of the Grail, we shouldn't make any wishes too hastily.

t_ch: 橡木家系真是后继有人啊，御主。不过，我觉得你的联手计划过于理想了。每位参战的主从都有自己想实现的愿望。
t_en: You're a worthy successor of the Oak Family, Master. But I think your plan for alliances is a bit idealistic. Each participant and their Servant have their own desires.

t_ch: 而在决出最后的胜者前，没人能保证，你信任的这位{NICKNAME}和{F#她}{M#他}的Saber不会对我们刀剑相向。
t_en: And until the final winner is decided, no one can guarantee that your trusted {NICKNAME} and {M#his}{F#her} Saber won't turn their blades on us.

t_ch: 你这话就有些过分了。
t_en: Hey! That's a bit harsh.

t_ch: 不过眼下，我依旧会听从你的调遣。但如果在和其他从者结盟议和时情况有任何变化，我都将作出我自己的决断。
t_en: But for now, I'll follow your lead. However, if the situation changes when trying to form alliances with other Servants, I'll make my own decisions.

t_ch: 咦？波提欧突然给我发了一条很长的短信。
t_en: Huh? Boothill just sent me a really long text message.

t_ch: 我也收到了。是群发的吗？
t_en: I got one too. Was it a mass text?

t_ch: 这是…波提欧先生发的短信吗？
t_en: Is this... a text from Mr. Boothill?

t_ch: 阿基维利在上，他骂得好脏！
t_en: For the love of Akivili, he's cursing up a storm!

t_ch: 啊？你已经能脑内自动转码他的脏话了吗？
t_en: Huh? You've already figured out how to automatically translate his swearing in your head?

t_ch: 不对不对，这压根就不是他写的吧！
t_en: Wait, wait, this absolutely isn't from him!

t_ch: 这根本就不是他写的吧？
t_en: This isn't from him at all, is it?

t_ch: 内容姑且不论，波提欧先生甚至不怎么擅长打字，更遑论这种书信格式了……
t_en: Putting the content aside, Boothill isn't exactly good at typing, let alone this kind of formal letter format...

t_ch: 看来…「相互厮杀」已经开始了。
t_en: Looks like... the "mutual slaughter" has already begun.

t_ch: 瞧，和平不过是战争的插叙，那位波提欧先生…恐怕已经被人击败了。
t_en: See, peace is just the interlude in a war. That Boothill... he's probably already been taken down.

t_ch: 不，他是位老练的战士，绝对不会这么早就退场。但一定有人拿到了他的手机，想利用他的身份引诱我们前往克劳克影视乐园。
t_en: No, he's a seasoned warrior. There's no way he's out this early. But someone must've gotten their hands on his phone and is using his identity to lure us to Clock Studios Theme Park.

t_ch: 这邀请函写得真郑重。盛情难却啊！我们得欣然赴约才行。
t_en: This invitation is written so earnestly. I guess we'll have to graciously accept.

t_ch: 嗯，我也是这么想的。如果现在赶过去，也许还有机会营救波提欧先生。你们怎么看？
t_en: Yeah, I was thinking the same. If we rush over now, there's still a chance to save Mr. Boothill. What do you all think?

t_ch: 我就喜欢直面陷阱。
t_en: I like diving right into the trap.

t_ch: 我也有此意。
t_en: Count me in too.

t_ch: 说实话，有点慌。
t_en: To be honest, I'm a bit on edge.

t_ch: 不必担心，我的御主。无论发生什么，我都会保护你的。
t_en: Don't worry, Master. No matter what happens, I'll protect you.

t_ch: 波提欧不会有事的！
t_en: Boothill will be fine!

t_ch: 我也相信他可以全身而退。无论如何，这都是一个和他结成同盟，停止厮杀的好机会。
t_en: I believe he'll make it out safely too. Either way, this is a good opportunity to ally with him and stop the fighting.

t_ch: 对方布置好了陷阱，如果放弃支援，固然不必承受风险。但失去两位盟友，在之后的袭击中我们反倒会陷入更不利的境地。
t_en: The enemy has set up a trap. If we pull back now, we avoid the risk. But losing two allies would put us at an even greater disadvantage in later attacks.

t_ch: 那就出发吧！
t_en: Then let's head out!

t_ch: 明明只是一场{RUBY_B#虚假的圣杯战争}似是而非的闹剧{RUBY_E#}，却有如此精妙的布置，这一点让我百思不得其解。
t_en: It's a {RUBY_B#False Holy Grail War}mockery of a war{RUBY_E#}, but you set it up so intricately... I don't get it.

t_ch: 隐喻时间变化的十二时刻，还有位居所有时刻中心的这枚{RUBY_B#大剧院}「月亮」{RUBY_E#}。
t_en: The Twelve Hours that referenced the passage of time, and this {RUBY_B#Grand Theater}"moon"{RUBY_E#} placed right at the heart of it all.

t_ch: 任何受过粗浅魔术训练的人都能感应到，力量正源源不断地聚流在此。但偏偏这个世界并没有所谓的「魔术」与「魔术师」。
t_en: Anyone with magecraft training can feel power continuously flowing into this place, but this world has no "magecraft" or "mages."

t_ch: 所以，这场圣杯战争的一切真的都出自你的手笔吗，老爷子？你看起来并没有什么魔术师的气质啊？
t_en: So, did you really design this whole Holy Grail War, old man? You don't exactly look like a mage.

t_ch: 嚯嗬嗬嗬！凭一点商人本能的敏锐，就会知道该在哪儿布局落子。而我需要知道的，圣杯都告诉了我。
t_en: Ohohoho! The sharp instincts of a businessman are enough for me to know where and when to make my moves, and the Holy Grail has told me everything I need to know.

t_ch: 你…是那位巡海游侠的从者，没想到，你的嗅觉比猎犬家系的爪牙还要敏锐啊！
t_en: You are the Servant of that Galaxy Ranger... I didn't think your nose would be even sharper than the Bloodhound Family's minions.

t_ch: 对了，你不回到御主身边为他忠心奋战，而是单骑突入，这样好吗？那个牛仔现在正身陷囹圄，急需你的拯救啊……
t_en: Is it really wise for you to charge in here on your own instead of loyally staying at your Master's side? That cowboy's in quite a pinch right now and would appreciate a speedy rescue...

t_ch: 真是不省心啊。要是我在这儿打爆你的脑袋，也就等于解救了我那位铁疙瘩御主，对吧？
t_en: What a pain. If I blow your head off right here, that'd save my metal clump of a Master too, no?

t_ch: 作为过来人，让我给你个忠告吧——圣杯从不会如人所愿，它能留下的，只有被欲望掩埋的亡灵。
t_en: Let me offer you some advice from experience — the Holy Grail never grants wishes the way people expect. All it leaves behind are dead souls buried by their desires.

t_ch: 嚯嗬嗬嗬！十分感谢阁下的告诫。所以，我这不正在为圣杯赋予新的价值吗？一档比许愿机更值得投资的娱乐节目。
t_en: Ohohoho! I appreciate the advice, truly. In that case, am I not giving the Holy Grail a new purpose? It'll become an entertainment spectacle much more worthy of investment than some wish-granting chalice.

t_ch: 哼，「娱乐节目」…原来如此，这玩意儿与圣杯相性不错，也难怪能启动这场圣杯战争。
t_en: Heh, "entertainment spectacle"... I get it now. That clicks pretty well with the essence of the Holy Grail. No wonder it managed to power this Holy Grail War.

t_ch: 我一直在想，在这个神秘衰退的世界里，如此贫瘠的土地上，就算手握圣杯，除了往里倒酒外什么也做不成吧？
t_en: I've been wondering what you could possibly put into the Holy Grail apart from wine, given this is a barren world where Mystics have completely degraded.

t_ch: 如果有什么东西能取代魔力，为圣杯灌注创造奇迹的热量…那就另当别论了。
t_en: But if something could replace magical energy, and manage to infuse the Holy Grail with enough power to create miracles... well, that changes things entirely.

t_ch: 原本应当保持「神秘的隐匿」的「圣杯战争」，摇身一变成了公开的娱乐节目。
t_en: Instead of something that must maintain the Concealment of Mystics, the Holy Grail War will become a public entertainment spectacle instead.

t_ch: 而这个浸泡在梦中的星球上，人们因为知晓圣杯的仪式、见证英灵的召唤，相信了圣杯奇迹的存在。
t_en: Then, the people on this dream-soaked planet will come to believe in the miracle of the Holy Grail through learning its rituals and witnessing the summoning of Heroic Spirits.

t_ch: 于是，他们点点滴滴的愿望化作了燃料，为圣杯镀上一层虚造的奇迹——而你所主持的这场闹剧，践踏了他们的愿望！
t_en: And so, little by little, their wishes will turn into fuel, coating the Holy Grail in a fabricated miracle — and the farce you've orchestrated will end up stomping all over those wishes!

t_ch: 真是了不得的见识，异界的从者，你的出现确实让我有些意外。但你们的到来是必要的，圣杯需要更高效的燃料，也就是你们——
t_en: Quite the impressive insight, Servant from another world. Your presence here was certainly a surprise to me, but you and your friends are needed. The Holy Grail needs more efficient fuel — and that means... you.

t_ch: 音符小姐，哦不，███，是时候向匹诺康尼展现你真正的魅力了。
t_en: Miss Note... No, ████. It's time for you to show Penacony your true charm.

t_ch: 幕布簌簌落下，掩去舞台上的一切。
t_en: The curtain fell with a soft rustle, veiling the stage in silence.

t_ch: 你要我做的事，我办到了……
t_en: I did what you wanted me to do...

t_ch: 辛苦了，老朋友。一眨眼这么多年过去了，我依然记得你在银幕上的每一场演出，跳过的每一段舞，唱出的每一支小调……
t_en: Well done, my old friend. So many years have passed, but I still remember every performance you gave, every dance you danced, every melody you sang...

t_ch: 那时候的你，就像一簇盛开的鸢尾花。不过，比起你正当盛年时的舞台风姿，我更喜欢如今这般模样…卸去了所有外在装饰，如此平凡的你。
t_en: You were like a blooming iris, but I prefer how you are now compared to your heydays on the stage... stripped of all the external pretenses, so simple, so ordinary.

t_ch: 够了，奥帝。过时的比喻就像隔夜餐点让我作呕。我已经履行了约定，到你了。
t_en: Enough, Oti. Outdated metaphors are as revolting as last night's leftovers. I've kept my promise. Now it's your turn.

t_ch: 不急，老朋友。你想要的一切根本用不着魔术和奇迹，用钱就能办到——所谓圣杯，也不过是用黄金浇铸的容器。
t_en: There's no rush, my old friend. Everything you desire can be obtained without magecraft or miracles — money is all it takes. The so-called Holy Grail is nothing more than a vessel crafted from gold.

t_ch: 而我们这出节目的主角们就要来了，你负责将他们拖住，直到仪式的最后一步完成。届时，我会履行约定的。
t_en: And the real stars of this show are about to make their entrance. Your job is to hold them off until the last step of the ritual is complete. Once that is done, I will honor my end of the deal.

t_ch: 奥帝，看来，你的商业头脑也跟这个世界一起腐烂了。
t_en: Oti, looks like your business acumen has rotted along with this world.

t_ch: 我连困住刚才那名从者都力不从心，你居然想让我打败他们所有人？
t_en: I could barely restrain that one Servant, and now you expect me to take them all on?

t_ch: 嚯嗬嗬嗬！别妄自菲薄，和葛瑞迪不同，你可是真正的艺术家！在舞台上以一人演绎万千角色悲喜人生的才能，你正是这些「英雄」的克星。
t_en: Ohohoho! Don't be so modest. Unlike Grady, you're a true artist! You had the talent to bring thousands of characters to life all on your own. You are the perfect counter to these "heroes."

t_ch: 我会将圣杯积累的力量托付给你，给予你压倒他们的筹码。如此一来，这场盛大的仪式必将迎来圆满的收场！
t_en: I will give you the power the Holy Grail has accumulated so you can crush them all. Then, this grand spectacle will come to a glorious finale!

t_ch: 他宝贝的，那个蓝色的哥们儿不是说先来探路吗？他怎么连个回报都没有，该不会给那老可爱算计了吧？
t_en: Fork it, wasn't that blue guy supposed to scout this place ahead of us? Haven't even heard a single peep from him so far... Could that old man have pulled a fast one on him?

t_ch: 没有战斗过的痕迹，不太像Lancer的作风。
t_en: No signs of battle. This doesn't quite seem like something Lancer would do.

t_ch: 没准儿去钓鱼了。
t_en: Maybe he went fishing.

t_ch: 是不是迷路了？
t_en: Or is he lost?

t_ch: 依照我对那个男人的了解，他甚至会挑起些没有意义的争执，然后心满意足地转身离开……
t_en: From what I know about that man, he might even stir up some pointless fights, just to walk away happy...

t_ch: 音符小姐！你没事就好——
t_en: Miss Note! I'm glad you're okay—

t_ch: 等等，诸位，情况似乎不太对劲。音符小姐，你在这儿见过Lancer了吧？
t_en: Wait. Something's off. Miss Note, I trust that you've seen Lancer here, haven't you?

t_ch: 不错，他来到大剧院，打算直奔主题，用他手中的长枪把奥帝先生钉在大幕上。我对他的看法深表赞同……
t_en: Yes. He came to the Grand Theater with his spear raised and ready, eager to impale Old Oti on the curtain. I couldn't agree more with his approach...

t_ch: 不过，获得这第二次生命实在不易，我还有未实现的愿望。因此，我请他稍事休息片刻。
t_en: But I still have dreams left unfulfilled in this miraculous second life... So, I asked him to take a break.

t_ch: 同时，我也不得不请各位小憩片刻……直到奥帝先生完成仪式为止。
t_en: And I must insist that every one of you take a break as well... until Mr. Oti finishes the ritual.

t_ch: 无需担心，不会有半点痛苦。请诸位剥离舞台上的炽烈激情与雄心壮志…就像帷幕后的演员们卸去脸上疲惫与妆容那样…好好休息。
t_en: Fear not. There will be no pain. Please leave your passion and ambitions on the stage, like how actors shed their fatigue and makeup behind the curtains... and rest.

t_ch: 这里是……？
t_en: Where's this...?

t_ch: 怎么可能…我为什么会出现在不列颠？
t_en: How... is this possible...? Why am I in Britain?

t_ch: 她把我们干哪儿来了？
t_en: Where did she send us?

t_ch: 这里是…你的故乡？
t_en: Is this... your homeland?

t_ch: 不错，我记得这儿…所有的一草一木。原野，风的气息，还有这座镇子……Caster想用这些记忆里的东西来困住我？
t_en: Yes. I remember it all... every tree, every blade of grass, the fields, the wind's scent, and this town... Does Caster think she can trap me with these memories?

t_ch: 哼，这座镇子可是我身为骑士的起点。
t_en: Hmph. This town is nothing less than where my life as a knight began.

t_ch: 哟！是阿尔托利斯啊！你也是来拔剑的吗？
t_en: Oh! Artorius! Here to draw the sword, aren't you?

t_ch: 既然你叫我「阿尔托利斯」…那么，你是艾登？
t_en: You're calling me Artorius...? Is it Aiden?

t_ch: 我就说嘛，那个叫梅林的魔术师吹得煞有介事，「将宝剑从石头里拔出的人会成为不列颠的王」。就算是见习骑士，也会忍不住想去试试。
t_en: I knew it. That mage Merlin spun such a tall tale, something about "Whosoe'er pulleth out this sword of this stone is rightwise king of all England." Even an apprentice knight wouldn't be able to resist that.

t_ch: 不过，像你这样的小个子，拔出选定之剑成为王，我实在是没法想象那样的情景啊。
t_en: That being said... A little fellow like you drawing the sword and becoming king? I really can't picture that.

t_ch: …说的也是，我也这么觉得。希望石中剑能选出正确的王。
t_en: ...Right. I agree. I hope the Sword in the Stone manages to pick the right king.

t_ch: 御主，我们走吧……
t_en: Master. Let's go.

t_ch: 他刚才叫你…
t_en: What did he just call you...?

t_ch: 阿尔托利斯，那是我的男名。毕竟在那个时代，不列颠的王若是女子身份，可不利于统治。
t_en: Artorius. That is the male name I used. After all, a female king would have had a very difficult time ruling Britain back in those days.

t_ch: 我们现在所身处的…应当是我拔出宝剑的前一刻。
t_en: We've returned to the moment right before I drew the sword from the stone.

t_ch: …我还以为Caster会准备什么怪物啊，异族的军队啊，巨龙啊之类的对手来困住我们。
t_en: ...I thought Caster would use monsters, foreign armies, or even dragons to trap us here.

t_ch: …真是怀念这一切。作为骑士离开村子后，我就再也没回来过。眼前的景色，是我还是个无名小卒时，那幼小、稚嫩的梦。
t_en: ...How I miss all this. I never returned here after I left as a knight. What we see before us is the childish dream I had when I was still just a nameless nobody.

t_ch: 少女像是被什么吸引住，眺望向道路的尽头。
t_en: The girl seemed drawn by something. Her gaze wandered to the end of the path.

t_ch: …可惜了，明明特意为我创造了这样的幻境，却连个坐骑也没有。
t_en: ...What a shame. She's created such a fantasy for me, yet I don't even have a mount.

t_ch: 你想要冲出幻境吗？
t_en: Do you want to break free from this dream?

t_ch: 是有想去的地方吗？
t_en: Is there somewhere you want to go?

t_ch: 我去给你找一匹践踏者来…
t_en: I'll find you a Trampler...

t_ch: 我小时候最大的梦想，就是拥有一匹自己的坐骑。想成为骑士，骑上马，沿着眼前的这条路前进，穿过原野一直到草原深处，艾克托的屋子就在那里。
t_en: My greatest dream as a child was to have a mount of my own. To become a knight. To ride a horse. To follow this very path across the fields and reach the grasslands where Ector's house lies.

t_ch: 艾克托是谁？
t_en: Who's Ector?

t_ch: 他是受托抚养我长大的老骑士，从某种意义上来说，他就像是我的「父亲」。
t_en: He's the old knight tasked with raising me... he was my "father" in a sense.

t_ch: 他总是喜欢板着一副严厉的面孔，但却又比谁都容易被逗乐…他总是对我念叨着希望我成为这片土地「理想的王」。
t_en: He always wore a stern expression but laughed at the smallest things... He always told me that he hoped I'd become the "ideal king" for this land.

t_ch: 可是，他最终还是没能见到我成为王者的样子……
t_en: But... in the end, he never got to see me become a king...

t_ch: 啊，抱歉，御主，突然絮絮叨叨地谈起了自己的过去。既然眼下有这样的机会，就不管什么幻觉啊梦境啊，我带你在故乡到处逛逛吧？
t_en: I'm sorry, Master. I got carried away talking about my past. Now that we're here, how about we set aside our worries about illusions and dreams, and I'll show you around my hometown instead?

t_ch: 在少女所注视的地方，一个小小的骑士玩偶倚靠在树下，仿佛正做着一场无限延续的美梦。
t_en: There, beneath a tree, a tiny knight doll rested, as if lost in a dream that would never end.

t_ch: 如果这就是Caster召唤出的士兵，应该是个非常强大的对手。
t_en: If this is the soldier summoned by Caster, they must be an incredibly powerful opponent.

t_ch: 和你的造型有些相似。
t_en: It looks a bit like you.

t_ch: 超古代邪神手办！
t_en: Whoa, that figurine looks totally rad!

t_ch: 这个木雕是你的玩具吗？
t_en: Was this wooden toy yours?

t_ch: 我从没见过这样的玩具。我人生的前十五年，大部分时间都花在了养马和练剑上。到了休息的时候，有个叫梅林的魔术师会来到梦中向我宣讲为王治国的教诲。
t_en: I've never seen a toy like this. My first fifteen years were mostly spent caring for horses and honing my swordsmanship. During my breaks, a mage named Merlin would visit me in my dreams, and teach me about ruling as a king.

t_ch: 御主你呢？你的童年是怎么度过的？我还从没有听你讲起过你自己的经历呢。
t_en: What about you, Master? How did you spend your childhood? You've never talked about your past.

t_ch: 其实，我没有上过学…
t_en: Actually, I never went to school...

t_ch: 是吗？可听你谈起各种外界的消息，却比卡美洛宫廷的吟游诗人更博学啊？
t_en: Really? But you sound more knowledgeable than the bards of Camelot when you talk about all the news from the outside world.

t_ch: 人类踏入了群星的时代，过去难以想象的知识也变得稀松平常起来了……
t_en: Humanity has entered the age of the stars. Wisdom that was once unimaginably arcane has now become common knowledge...

t_ch: 关于过去，我也记得的不多…
t_en: I... don't remember a lot about my past...

t_ch: 我依稀能记得的，自己在一座空间站中被人唤醒了。
t_en: The only thing I remember is being woken up by someone in a space station.

t_ch: 是吗…难怪，御主的性格有些随心所欲啊，是因为没有过去的束缚吗？
t_en: Really? ...No wonder your personality is so carefree. Is it because you don't have a past holding you back, Master?

t_ch: 从我真正记事开始，我登上了一辆开往群星的列车，有了许多志同道合的朋友。虽然没有过去，但我有未来。
t_en: My earliest memory was boarding a train headed for the stars and meeting many kindred spirits as friends. Though I have no past, I have a future.

t_ch: 我很幸运。
t_en: I'm really lucky.

t_ch: 我很幸运。
t_en: I'm really lucky.

t_ch: 我很自由。
t_en: I'm totally free.

t_ch: 我很自由。
t_en: I'm totally free.

t_ch: 我成为了我想成为的人。
t_en: I became the person I wanted to be.

t_ch: 我成为了我想成为的人。
t_en: I became the person I wanted to be.

t_ch: 这真是…就算遭逢困苦，你也还是继续着自己美好的旅程，如果生前的我也能这样就好了。
t_en: Well... Even in the face of hardship, you are still continuing on your wonderful journey. I wish I did that in my life.

t_ch: 曾经，我也想成为某个人。我想挽救我脚下的这片土地和居住在其中的人们，免受化身为白龙的卑王伏提庚的荼毒，我想将人们从异族的铁蹄统治下解放出来。
t_en: Once upon a time, I also wanted to become a specific person. I wanted to save the land I stood on and the people who lived upon it. I wanted to repel the tyranny of King Vortigern the White Dragon. I wanted to deliver my people from the foreign invaders' hostile rule.

t_ch: 我想成为万民所期望的，拯救不列颠的理想之王。为此，我可以献上一切。
t_en: I wanted to be the ideal king my people dreamed of, someone who would save Britain. For that... I would have sacrificed everything.

t_ch: 魔术师告诉我，拔出石头上的选定之剑，我就能如愿。所有人都以为，不列颠会因此得救。
t_en: The mage told me that if I could pull the sword from the stone, then I would achieve my wish. Everyone thought that I would save Britain.

t_ch: 不列颠因此得救了吗？
t_en: So was Britain saved?

t_ch: 没有永恒的王权…
t_en: But no kingdom lasts forever...

t_ch: 没有。
t_en: I didn't save Britain.

t_ch: 我曾相信当我斩杀伏提庚，驱逐异族，召集圆桌骑士，建成坚不可摧的卡美洛城时，和平的乐土会降临在不列颠。
t_en: I once believed that once I slew Vortigern, expelled the foreign invaders, gathered the Knights of the Round Table, and built the impenetrable castle of Camelot, peace would finally come to Britain.

t_ch: 但那不过是短暂的幻梦，麾下的骑士们离我而去，其中还有人向我兴兵挑战…不列颠又陷入了战火之中。
t_en: But that was all a fleeting dream. The knights who once served me abandoned me, and some even raised armies to challenge my rule... Britain was once again engulfed in war.

t_ch: 在拔出选王之剑前，梅林就向我揭示过这一切……
t_en: Merlin had already warned me of all this before I drew the Sword of Selection...

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>他说，一旦拔出剑，就会迎来被所有人憎恶的可悲结局。</color>
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>He told me that once I drew the sword, I would meet a tragic end, hated by all.</color>

t_ch: 在国家最繁荣的时刻，他就曾告诉过我……
t_en: And while the kingdom was in its prime, he also told me...

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>不列颠总有一天会结束，而它此刻的繁荣，建立在王的痛苦之上。</color>
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>That Britain would one day fall, and its current prosperity was built on the suffering of its king.</color>

t_ch: 我以为，如果我能背负起国家的苦难，换来人民的乐土，即便迎来悲哀的结局，一切也都值得。
t_en: Yet I thought... I could carry the weight of the kingdom's pain and trade it for the peace of the people, the sacrifice would be worth it, and I would be willing to face my thankless end.

t_ch: 你让我想起了星期日…
t_en: You remind me of Sunday...

t_ch: 你向Saber讲述了发生在匹诺康尼谐乐大典上的一场迷梦：一个男人，妄想以一己之身维系地上天国。
t_en: You recount the fantastical dream you had during Penacony's Charmony Festival to Saber, how a man dreamed of creating a paradise kingdom with his own power.

t_ch: 知更鸟小姐的兄长…确实，我当时的所想与所为，与他何其相似。
t_en: Miss Robin's older brother? His thoughts and actions sound a lot similar to mine.

t_ch: 你的愿望，只有圣杯能实现…
t_en: The only thing that can grant your wish would be the Holy Grail...

t_ch: 某位王以一己之身，将自己奉上神坛作为祭品，去兑现人间乐土的大梦。这与匹诺康尼的谐乐大典上所发生的一切何其相似。
t_en: Once, a king offered themself upon the altar — a lone sacrifice in exchange for a dream of Paradise on Earth. How eerily similar it all feels... to what unfolded during the Charmony Festival in Penacony.

t_ch: 可当我信誓旦旦向所有人描绘了理想之国，最后却只在不列颠的国土上留下无休止的战争、流血的战士和饥饿的孩子。
t_en: I promised everyone that I would build the ideal kingdom, but I left the land of Britain with endless wars, dying warriors, and starving children.

t_ch: 迎来毁灭的应该是我，而不是我的国家和人民，本该是这样才对。
t_en: The one who deserves to face destruction is me, not my country or my people. That's how it should have been.

t_ch: 这就是…石中剑吗？
t_en: So this is the Sword in the Stone...?

t_ch: 我曾经无数次想象，如果没有拔出这把剑，如果有比我更适合成为王的人选……
t_en: I've wondered countless times... had I not drawn this sword, or had there been someone more suited to be king...

t_ch: 如果我没有成为王，不列颠会成为真正的理想国吗？我又会过上怎样的生活？
t_en: Would Britain have truly become the utopia I dreamed of? And what kind of a life would I have lived?

t_ch: 拔出选定之剑，成为王；或者放弃，留在故乡，安度余生。
t_en: To pull out the sword and become king, or to give up and stay in my homeland to quietly live out my days.

t_ch: 现在，她将我带回了做出选择的时刻……
t_en: She's brought me back to the moment I made my choice...

t_ch: Saber，难不成你想留下来吗？
t_en: Saber, do you... wish to stay?

t_ch: 御主，我只是…有些疲惫。
t_en: Master... I'm so... tired.

t_ch: 好想能拥有一匹坐骑…在太阳落下前，骑上它穿过这片原野……
t_en: I wish I had a horse... to ride across these fields before the sun sets...

t_ch: Saber——！
t_en: Saber—!

t_ch: 你高声呼唤，但拖长的尾音并未传达到从者的耳中，当她在做出选择的刹那心生犹豫时，一切已经注定——
t_en: You called out, your voice echoing, but the drawn-out words never reached the Servant. In the moment she hesitated, fate had already been sealed—

t_ch: 事实上，从一开始你就不存在于这里。停留此处的，不过是坠入宝具中的你，在别人的梦里残留的一道虚影。
t_en: In truth... you were never here at all. What remains in this place is merely a shadow of you who fell into a Noble Phantasm, lingering in the dream of another.

t_ch: 她不过是个平凡无奇的女孩。扮演国王已经够痛苦了，为什么还要强迫她继续在英灵的幻觉中强撑下去呢？
t_en: She was nothing more than an ordinary girl. Playing the part of a king was already painful enough. Why should we force her to continue in the delusion known as Heroic Spirits?

t_ch: 不如丢下宝剑，舍弃荣耀，成为台下欣赏他人悲喜的凡夫俗子。
t_en: Better to cast aside her sword, abandon her glory, and become a nameless nobody who watches the world's grand show on the sidelines.

t_ch: 我将帮助各位一点一点卸去「英雄」的角色妆容，直到抹消你们最初的选择，也抹消一路所受的苦难。
t_en: I'll help all of you to remove the hero's garish makeup bit by bit, until I've wiped away even the choice you've made to become a hero, until I've wiped away all the pain you've endured on this thorny road.

t_ch: 我为所有人安排了一出，属于你们的，平庸而幸福的人生——你也不例外，{NICKNAME}。
t_en: I've prepared a simple and happy life for all of you in this show — Including you, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 所以——
t_en: Now —

t_ch: 所以，你要留下来吗？新来的。
t_en: So, newcomer, are you staying?

t_ch: 像你这样的家伙，能挤进黑塔空间站就已经耗尽人生所有的好运了，但我还是象征性地确认一下。
t_en: Someone like you has probably already burned through all your luck just trying to get into the Herta Space Station, but I'll go through the motions and confirm it anyway.

t_ch: 要知道，地概科的工作可没你想象中轻松。
t_en: Just so you know, working at the Department of Cosmic Geography isn't as easy as you think.

t_ch: 你看着我再说一遍？
t_en: Look at me in the eyes and say that again.

t_ch: 她把我干哪儿来了？
t_en: Where did she send me???

t_ch: 谁？黑塔女士？这儿当然是她的空间站啊。
t_en: Who? Madam Herta? Well, this IS her space station.

t_ch: 不认识我？你才是新来的吧？
t_en: You don't know who I am? Guess you're the real newcomer here.

t_ch: 哼，我刚来的时候，也跟你一样觉得自己特牛特了不起。
t_en: Hmph. Look. I thought I was pretty impressive too when I first arrived.

t_ch: 过段日子你就会明白，在这里，你只是个普普通通的科员，你自以为的那些聪明才智，在别人眼里啥也不是。
t_en: But you'll be beaten into shape in no time. You're just a regular researcher on this station. No one's gonna even bat an eye at your so-called talents and smarts.

t_ch: 我可是银河球棒侠！
t_en: But I'm the Galactic Baseballer!

t_ch: 就这？我还是地概科研侠呢。
t_en: And? I'm the Cosmic Geographer.

t_ch: 我打败过塔塔洛夫。
t_en: But I defeated Tatalov.

t_ch: 那是啥玩意儿？游戏里的角色？
t_en: Who's that? A game character?

t_ch: 我是无名客！我不属于这儿！
t_en: But I'm a Nameless! I don't belong here!

t_ch: ……你是否清醒？要不要请假去医疗科看看脑子？
t_en: ... Are you dreaming or something? Maybe you should take a sick day and get a check-up at the Medical Department.

t_ch: 说起来我也算半个星穹列车粉丝，我知道搭乘它的无名客各个都经历过了不起的冒险。你倒是说说看，你有啥丰功伟绩？
t_en: I'm a bit of a fan of the Astral Express, and I know that every Nameless who boards it has been through some incredible adventures. So, tell me. What great deeds have you done?

t_ch: 我脸接过末日兽大招！
t_en: I facetanked the Doomsday Beast's Ultimate!

t_ch: 哎，这个我有听过！听说空间站有个藏品被偷走了，被某个来历不明的闯入者吞进了肚子，还因此引来了反物质军团的袭击。
t_en: Oh, I've heard of that! Apparently, a valuable relic was stolen from the space station. It was swallowed by a mysterious intruder, which then led to an attack from the Antimatter Legion.

t_ch: 要不是黑塔女士出手解决了这场危机，咱们这都化灰了啊。你知道那个身份不明的闯入者是谁吗？
t_en: If it weren't for Madam Herta stepping in to handle the crisis, we'd all be burnt to a crisp by now. Any idea who that mysterious intruder was?

t_ch: 没错，我就不卖关子了，就是{M#小爷}{F#小姐}您啊。是艾丝妲站长法外开恩，把你送来了这儿找份工作。
t_en: Yep, you guessed it. It was you, {M#brat}{F#lass}. The only reason you got a job here is that Lead Researcher Asta took pity on you.

t_ch: 我在仙舟击退了幻胧！
t_en: I defeated Phantylia on the Xianzhou!

t_ch: 你是说不久前出现在仙舟大闹了一场的绝灭大君？
t_en: You mean the Lord Ravager who recently appeared on the Xianzhou and caused a huge ruckus?

t_ch: 据我最近听到的消息，她的计划被罗浮将军挫败了…但仙舟人也付出了巨大的代价。好心提醒你一句，别拿人家的灾难当成自己吹牛的谈资。
t_en: From what I've heard, her plan was foiled by the General of the Luofu... but the Xianzhou people paid a heavy price for it. Listen, don't use someone else's tragedy as your bragging material.

t_ch: 我把匹诺康尼从永眠中唤醒！
t_en: I woke Penacony from its eternal slumber!

t_ch: 让我猜猜，接下来你是不是要说，你被邀请去参加了谐乐大典？还在匹诺康尼成了盛会巨星？
t_en: Let me guess, next you'll say you were invited to the Charmony Festival? And became the Festive Superstar?

t_ch: 太对了，哥。
t_en: Right on, bro!

t_ch: 你是不是看过剧本？
t_en: Did you peek at the script?

t_ch: 我还成了那里的股东呢！
t_en: I even became a shareholder there!

t_ch: 我，我无话可说…
t_en: I... I don't know what to say...

t_ch: 哎呀，我早该猜到的！最近应物科进口了一批梦泡，有不少没见过世面的年轻科员都整天沉迷在里面，连工作都甩到一边去了。
t_en: Haha. Oh, I know what's happening now! The Department of Implement Arts imported a batch of dream bubbles. Loads of young naïve researchers have been wasting entire days in them, ignoring even their paid work.

t_ch: 想必你也是忆质入脑，幻觉缠身吧？不然怎么会觉得自己成了列车上的无名客呢？
t_en: You probably ate too much of that memoria too and thought those illusions were real. Why else would you believe you're a Nameless aboard the Express?

t_ch: 不过…我也不是不能理解。我听说列车本来邀请你和他们一起上路的，可是你拒绝了。怎么，现在后悔啦？
t_en: Well... I can't say I blame you. I heard the Express did invite you to join them, but you turned them down. Are you regretting that choice now?

t_ch: 我上车了啊！
t_en: But I DID board the Express!

t_ch: 你试着向眼前的科员呐喊，但你的声音却颤抖着给出了截然不同的回答。
t_en: You try to yell at the researcher before you, but your wavering voice gives a different response.

t_ch: 我害怕了。
t_en: I became afraid.

t_ch: 我…害怕了。
t_en: I became... afraid.

t_ch: 我不知道。
t_en: I don't know.

t_ch: 我…我不知道。
t_en: I... don't know...

t_ch: 我后悔了。
t_en: Yes, I regret my choice.

t_ch: 我…后悔了。
t_en: I... regret my choice.

t_ch: 这句回答是你的心中所想吗？还是某种虚假的操弄？你试着回想过去发生的一切，但它们变得愈发不真实。
t_en: But, was that answer truly what you thought? Or was it some sort of illusive manipulation? You try to piece together what has happened, but your memories feel more distant with every passing second.

t_ch: 关于星穹列车，关于那些无名客，记忆正像海水冲刷下的沙塔，四处溃散。你理解了Saber想要停留片刻的感受。
t_en: About the Astral Express and the Nameless. Like sand under the waves, your memories crumble and fade. And suddenly, you understand — why Saber chose to stay, even just for a moment.

t_ch: 为什么当时一念之差你没有登上星穹列车呢？这不重要了。眼前的地概科员看着你，也许是在组织安慰的措辞，也许是在咂摸几句玩笑带过这尴尬的凝重气氛。到最后，他开口了——
t_en: Why didn't you step aboard the Astral Express back then, on that split-second impulse? It no longer matters. The researcher in the Department of Cosmic Geography standing before you watches you closely, perhaps searching for words of comfort, or maybe looking for the right joke to shatter the heavy silence. Eventually, he speaks.

t_ch: 看你的简历似乎没什么特长，我先给你安排点轻松的工作好了……
t_en: Well, your resume doesn't show any particular skills, so I'll start you off with some light tasks...

t_ch: 整理地概科过去半年的经费报表，预约未来一个月的研究会议，收集各级科员的课题申请…够你干上一段时间了。
t_en: Sort the Department of Cosmic Geography's expenditure report from the past six months. Schedule research meetings for the upcoming month. Collect project proposals from all levels of researchers. Yeah, that should keep you busy for a while.

t_ch: 我真是研究员？
t_en: Am I really a researcher?

t_ch: 要不然呢？你总不可能是黑塔吧？
t_en: What else would you be? Madam Herta?

t_ch: 这不是地概科吗？
t_en: Isn't this the Department of Cosmic Geography?

t_ch: 别大惊小怪，在空间站，我们地概科是食物链的底端，这种事情都是家常便饭啦。
t_en: Don't be so surprised. Our department is at the bottom of the food chain on the station... this work happens all the time.

t_ch: 我走了，{NICKNAME}，派给你的活儿好好干啊。
t_en: I'm off, {NICKNAME}. Be sure to do your job well.

t_ch: 他打算离开，又突然不忍心地转身看向你。
t_en: He turns to leave... yet could not help himself from looking back at you.

t_ch: 我偶尔也会后悔自己十四岁那年没去进修「量子历史」而选择了「寰宇地概」，我当时觉得…数学太难了。我常常问自己，如果我做了别的选择，还会是今天这幅衰样吗？
t_en: Yeah, sometimes I regret picking the Geography of the Universe course when I was fourteen and walking away from Quantum History... I felt math was just too tough for me back then. But now, I ask myself... Would I still be stuck in this dead-end job if I made a different choice?

t_ch: 嗐，都是瞎想。新来的，别为已经做出的决定后悔。你看我，这样不挺好？
t_en: No no no. C'mon. Don't worry about the what-ifs. Why waste time regretting a choice you've already made? Look at me, things aren't so bad here, are they?

t_ch: 一点也不好！你内心发出嘶吼，但愤怒稍纵即逝。
t_en: This is all wrong! You scream internally, but the fury is fleeting.

t_ch: 男人满脸无奈地看着你，努力把嘴边的吐槽憋了回去。
t_en: The man looks at you with exasperation written all over his face, visibly biting back a snarky remark.

t_ch: 你迟到了，新人。再晚来几个系统日，报销时限都要过了。
t_en: You're late, newcomer. A few more system days of delay and we'll miss the reimbursement deadline.

t_ch: 看在你初来乍到的份上，就不为难你，赶紧干活吧。
t_en: I'll cut you some slack given that you're new. Now get to work.

t_ch: （好乱的桌子，是要我把这些全都整理干净吗？该从哪儿开始呢？）
t_en: (What a messy desk. Am I supposed to organize all this stuff? Where do I even start?)

t_ch: 根据额度分类整理。
t_en: Sort by budget category.

t_ch: 根据时间分类整理。
t_en: Sort by timeline.

t_ch: 根据心情分类整理。
t_en: Sort by mood.

t_ch: 太慢了，像你这样搞，等到琥珀王敲下一锤都弄不完。
t_en: You're too slow. Keep going like this and you'll still be here when the Amber Lord makes THEIR next hammer swing.

t_ch: 怎么还蹬鼻子上脸了？
t_en: Don't push your luck.

t_ch: 我现在先给你一锤子！
t_en: I see you're itching for a fight!

t_ch: 你怎么不自己来做？
t_en: Why don't you do it yourself?

t_ch: 你正欲破口大骂，但内心却不由地生出一丝怀疑。
t_en: You're on the verge of shouting, but just then, a sliver of doubt creeps inside you.

t_ch: 这真是你想做的吗？放轻松，不要那么粗暴，想想你真正应该做的是什么？
t_en: Is this really what you want? Take a breath. Don't lash out. Focus on what you truly need to do.

t_ch: 掏出球棒给她一顿打。
t_en: Take out the baseball bat and give her a good beating.

t_ch: 你发现自己总能随手掏出的球棒早已不在手边。话说回来，你真的曾拥有过那根球棒吗？
t_en: The bat you always have at your side... is no longer there. Then again, did you ever really have it in the first place?

t_ch: 撂挑子不干了。
t_en: I quit.

t_ch: 那么，你要去哪里？你又能去哪里？像你这样并无特长的年轻人不正该找个十六险一金的稳定工作，舒坦地干到世界末日？
t_en: But where would you go? Is there anywhere you can go? You're young, with no special talents. Isn't your best bet a cushy job with full benefits, clocking in every day until the world ends?

t_ch: 完成普通的工作。
t_en: Complete the basic tasks.

t_ch: 还愣着做什么？以为会有太空仿生田螺替你干活吗？
t_en: What are you standing around for? You think space bionic snails are going to do the work for you?

t_ch: 根据额度分类整理。
t_en: Sort by budget category.

t_ch: 根据时间分类整理。
t_en: Sort by timeline.

t_ch: 根据心情分类整理。
t_en: Sort by mood.

t_ch: 没错，就是这样，平静，且美好地放下双手。现在，你已经对工作轻车熟路了，把剩下的文件也整理干净吧。
t_en: Yes. That's it. Calmly and nicely, drop the fight. By now, the job feels like second nature. Might as well tidy up the last of the files.

t_ch: 如果你想说「我们是不是在哪儿见过」，那我可以回答你，「没有」。
t_en: If you're going to ask me "Have we met before," my answer is gonna be "no."

t_ch: 可我真的感觉我们有…
t_en: But I really feel like we've...

t_ch: 好精准的预判！
t_en: What clairvoyance!

t_ch: 你看你明明就认识我。
t_en: See? You definitely know me.

t_ch: 你读了读他的工牌，上面除了一个言简意赅的「A」字别无其他。所属科是「家政科」…话说回来，空间站有这一科吗？
t_en: You glance at his work tag. On it, just a single letter — A. Department: Domestic Services. Wait... since when does the space station have a Domestic Services Department?

t_ch: 说起来，A叔，你难道不觉得，自己不属于这里吗？
t_en: C'mon, Uncle A, don't you ever feel like you don't belong here?

t_ch: 我梦见自己参与了圣杯战争。
t_en: I dreamt that I was part of the Holy Grail War.

t_ch: 你应该是被召唤的英灵。
t_en: You should've been one of the summoned Heroic Spirits.

t_ch: 我们不该在这里！
t_en: We shouldn't be here!

t_ch: 我明白，我明白的。每天都有一打科员跑来和我聊天，他们都宣布自己不属于这里。
t_en: I get it. I really do. Dozens of researchers come up to me every day, all claiming they don't belong here.

t_ch: 因为现在的生活不称心如意，就觉得自己应该属于其他地方，这不过是另一种美化了的逃避现实的借口罢了。
t_en: They think they should be somewhere else because life isn't going their way. It's just another dressed-up excuse to escape reality.

t_ch: 即使逃去其他地方，只要后退的本性不改变，迟早有一天会无处可去的。
t_en: As long as your instinct is just to back down and run, you'll run out of places to go even if you manage to get out of this place.

t_ch: 依我看，这里不挺好的嘛。没有世界危机，没有想要毁灭人类的怪胎。厨房也很方便，除了食材有点古怪。
t_en: Personally? I think this place isn't so bad. No world-threatening crises, no freaks trying to end humanity. The kitchen's convenient, too... though the ingredients are a bit odd.

t_ch: 虽然照样有个「挥宝石如土」的小姑娘，对绝大多数人也算是理想乡了。
t_en: We've still got a little girl who throws around gemstones like dirt, but it's pretty much a utopia for most people.

t_ch: 平凡的人生……等离开了再后悔，就来不及了。
t_en: There's no turning back once you leave your ordinary life behind.

t_ch: 你看了看他，欲言又止。
t_en: You glance at him, wanting to say something, but decided not to.

t_ch: 打消念头了？我就说嘛，你没那么想离开这儿，对吧？
t_en: Changed your mind, huh? I knew it. You don't really want to leave, do you?

t_ch: 花了好几天的时间，你终于完成了漫长的整理工作。
t_en: Days pass. After long hours of sorting, you're finally done with the work.

t_ch: 虽然违和感仍然在你的心中徘徊，但在解决它之前，还是先休息一会儿吧。
t_en: Although an uncanny feeling lingers in your chest, before it's resolved, you should take a break.

t_ch: 飘浮，静静地在梦的表面，维持在沉没前的瞬间。
t_en: Drifting quietly over the surface of a dream, suspended in the moment before you sink.

t_ch: 嗯，让我们来说说王的故事吧，一位名为尤瑟的王。
t_en: Let me tell you a story about a king... a king named Uther.

t_ch: 在我到来之前，他曾统领不列颠的子民。而在我到来之后，为了捍卫不列颠的子民，他向摧毁一切的白龙发起进攻，以败北告终。
t_en: He ruled the people of Britain before I showed up. And after my appearance, he launched an attack against the White Dragon to protect his people, a battle that ended in defeat.

t_ch: 可即便如此，人们仍称他为最伟大的国王。在你看来，是什么令他成为了英雄？
t_en: Even so, people still called him the greatest king. What do you think made him a hero?

t_ch: 身为国王的荣耀。
t_en: The innate glory of being a king.

t_ch: 不，和他相同的各个部族的王，他们的名字都没能流传下去。
t_en: No. The names of kings from other tribes, kings equal to his stature, have all been forgotten.

t_ch: 身为骑士的功绩。
t_en: The deeds he achieved as a knight.

t_ch: 不，王的身边有比他更英勇的骑士，他们的名字都没能流传下去。
t_en: No. There were knights more courageous than him by his side, but their names didn't survive in time.

t_ch: 身为人的勇气。
t_en: The courage he exhibited as a human.

t_ch: 举剑挑战自己绝对无法战胜的龙，剑折弓断，身首异处，他身为人类的一切注定是失败的……
t_en: He raised his sword to challenge a dragon he knew he could never defeat. The sword shattered, the bow snapped, and he was beheaded. As a human, everything about him was destined to fail...

t_ch: 但他为了拯救什么而举起剑的模样，却在不列颠的至黑之夜里留下了一颗星星。
t_en: But the way he raised his sword to bring deliverance left a shining star in Britain's darkest nights.

t_ch: 我们所目睹的星星虽然并非它们本来的样子，但光却不是谎言，它会照耀很久很久，直到照亮下一位英雄的道路。
t_en: The stars we see may not be their original forms, but their light isn't a lie. It will shine for ages, lighting the way for the next hero.

t_ch: 舞台，那位{RUBY_B#音符小姐}失去名字的英灵{RUBY_E#}如此称呼我们的人生，她用宝具抹去了星星的辉光。
t_en: That {RUBY_B#Miss Note}Heroic Spirit who lost her name{RUBY_E#} referred to our lives as "a stage," and she used her Noble Phantasm to erase the star's radiance.

t_ch: 但…御主，如果你仍然保有勇气…如果你愿意成为星星，所有目视你的观众也都将登上舞台，成为英雄。
t_en: But Master... if you still have courage... if you're still willing to become that star, then all the spectators who have seen you will also step onto the stage, becoming heroes themselves.

t_ch: 不知不觉间说了些大道理，虽然只是对梅林的蹩脚模仿。
t_en: Look at me. Talking big all of a sudden and doing a bad imitation of Merlin.

t_ch: 你在哪儿？
t_en: Where are you?

t_ch: 我在等待一个即将登台演出的人，也在骑马穿越荒野。梦就要结束了，御主，无论是对我，还是对你。
t_en: I'm waiting for someone who's about to take the stage. I'm also riding through the wilderness. The dream is nearing its end, Master, for me and you.

t_ch: 呦，{F#姐们儿}{M#小哥}，如果你也是来这里偷懒的，这儿已经满员了。
t_en: Hey, {M#buddy.}{F#lass.} If you're here to slack off, you're a bit too late. This place is already at full capacity.

t_ch: 我是来上班的。
t_en: I'm here to work.

t_ch: 我可是模范科员。
t_en: I'm a model researcher employee.

t_ch: 哈哈哈哈，那我就是模范钓客了。
t_en: Hahaha, then I guess I'm the model fisherman.

t_ch: 我这就举报你。
t_en: I'm reporting you!

t_ch: 科长那小子，正忙着和逃跑的阮•梅造物斗智斗勇呢，可没时间管我。
t_en: The chief is too busy chasing those Ruan Mei Creations that ran away. He won't have time to read your report.

t_ch: 没事儿，你在这儿干活，我在这儿摸鱼，咱们各取所需。
t_en: Well, no worries. You work here, and I'll slack off. We each get what we need.

t_ch: 暂且无视这位吊儿郎当的防卫科科员，你低下头开始工作。加油，不要输给这种无所事事的大人！
t_en: For now, you ignore the Security Department staff's careless attitude and lower your head to resume your task. You've got this! You're not losing to some grown-up who's slacking all day!

t_ch: 首先，《关于噬硅菌群对行星磁场干涉的定量分析》会议。参会人数<unbreak>15</unbreak>人，会议时间在未来两天内即可。
t_en: First on the list: A Quantitative Analysis of Mycophage Cluster Interference on Planetary Magnetic Fields. Fifteen attendees. Must be scheduled within the next two days.

t_ch: 今天上午。
t_en: This morning.

t_ch: 抱歉，您所预约的时间已无会议室。
t_en: Sorry, there are no conference rooms available at your requested time.

t_ch: 明天下午。
t_en: The next afternoon.

t_ch: 抱歉，您所预约的时间已无会议室。
t_en: Sorry, there are no conference rooms available at your requested time.

t_ch: 后天晚上。
t_en: The night after the next day.

t_ch: 抱歉，你所预约的时间已无会议室。
t_en: Sorry, there are no conference rooms available at your requested time.

t_ch: 哎，居然连晚上的会议室都预约空了，这里的人可真是勤勉。
t_en: Ugh, the conference rooms are fully booked even at night. People here really are workaholics.

t_ch: 我想起来了！你就是他们说的星核精？话说，你睡觉的时候，会像萤火虫一样发光吗？
t_en: Ah! Wait! You're the Stellaron they were talking about, huh? Tell me... when you sleep, do you glow like a firefly?

t_ch: 要我说，像你这样的年轻人，就该找个师傅学上一身本事，闯出自己的天地，像个英雄那样，无拘无束。
t_en: Young ones like you should really find a mentor, learn some skills, and carve your own path. Live like a true hero, no strings attached.

t_ch: 别在意耳边的干扰，看看下一场会议的需求。《星震现象对科员艺术创作的影响》，参会人数2人，会议时间不限。
t_en: Ignore the distractions around you. Let's look at the next meeting's details: The Effects of Starquake Phenomena on the Artistic Expression of Researchers. Two attendees, no time constraints.

t_ch: 一年后。
t_en: One year later.

t_ch: 十年后。
t_en: Ten years later.

t_ch: 百年后。
t_en: A century later.

t_ch: 你似乎想起了什么，在空间站外划过的引擎火花，列车跃迁开始前的轻微失重…等等，这些全都是不存在的空想，科员，你应该把精力放到现在的工作上。
t_en: Something stirs in your memory, sparks from an engine streaking past the space station, the faint weightlessness before the Express conducts a warp jump... Wait, no. None of that is real. Researcher, you should be focusing on your task at hand.

t_ch: 还有<unbreak>899</unbreak>个需要进行预约的会议，在把事情搞砸前，最好快一点完成它们。
t_en: You've still got <unbreak>899</unbreak> more meetings to schedule. You'd better hurry up and finish before things spiral out of control.

t_ch: 人造矿石研讨。
t_en: Man-Made Ore Symposium.

t_ch: 博识学会来访。
t_en: Visit from the Intelligentsia Guild.

t_ch: 月度经费审批。
t_en: Monthly Budget Review.

t_ch: 行星衰亡观测。
t_en: Planetary Decline Observation.

t_ch: 很好，算上系统新增的两个会议需求，再完成<unbreak>897</unbreak>个会议的预约就能结束了。
t_en: Alright. Including the two new system-generated meetings, just <unbreak>897</unbreak> more to go, and the list is done.

t_ch: 天然矿石研讨。
t_en: Natural Ore Symposium.

t_ch: 公司专员来访。
t_en: Visit from IPC Specialists.

t_ch: 月度经费审批。
t_en: Monthly Budget Review.

t_ch: 恒星坍缩观测。
t_en: Stellar Collapse Observation.

t_ch: 没错，就是这样。
t_en: That's right, exactly.

t_ch: 你已经对眼前的工作得心应手。
t_en: You're in the zone now, handling the work before you with practiced ease.

t_ch: █星核██研讨。
t_en: █Stellaron██ Symposium.

t_ch: ███列车来访。
t_en: Visit from ███ Express.

t_ch: █奇物██审批。
t_en: █Curio██ Approval.

t_ch: ██自我█观测。
t_en: ██Self█ Observation.

t_ch: 说起来，{F#姐们儿}{M#小哥}，你对钓鱼有兴趣吗？别看外面都是一片真空，能钓起来的玩意儿可不少。
t_en: You ever tried fishing, {M#buddy?}{F#lass?} Don't be fooled by the vacuum outside. There's still plenty to catch.

t_ch: 迁徙的无形鲢，会和钓客搏斗的硅基软骨鱼，上次我甚至还钓到过一只扑满。
t_en: There's the migrating invisible carp, the silicon-based soft-boned fish that put up a fight. I even caught a Trotter last time.

t_ch: 虽然你的精神已十分疲惫，但男人的神采仍打动了你。忙里偷闲的娱乐，一根钓竿，空间站的生活也没你想象中的那么不堪。
t_en: Although you are exhausted beyond measure, the man's expression moves you. Your entertainment during a momentary respite from work, a fishing rod. Life at the space station isn't as bleak as you thought.

t_ch: 也许，这里就是你的理想乡。
t_en: Maybe, this is your utopia.

t_ch: 对了，听说星穹列车又来了，会在空间站停靠一段时间。要不要过去看看？就当是凑热闹。
t_en: By the way, I heard the Astral Express is coming back and will be stopping at the space station for a while. Want to go check it out? Just for fun.

t_ch: 好久不见，{NICKNAME}，在空间站过得还好吗？
t_en: Long time no see, {NICKNAME}. How's life been on the space station?

t_ch: 好得不得了。
t_en: Great. Couldn't be better.

t_ch: 看到你能适应这里的生活，我也就放心了。
t_en: I'm glad to see you've settled in here.

t_ch: 不太好。
t_en: Not so well.

t_ch: 这样啊，有困难的话，找艾丝妲聊聊吧，相信她会帮你解决的。
t_en: Oh, I see... If things are tough, you could always talk to Asta. She's sure to help you out.

t_ch: 非常痛苦。
t_en: It's been... really bad.

t_ch: 这样啊，有困难的话，找艾丝妲聊聊吧，相信她会帮你解决的。
t_en: Oh, I see... If things are tough, you could always talk to Asta. She's sure to help you out.

t_ch: 上次告别之后，我们又去了很多地方，贝洛伯格，仙舟罗浮，匹诺康尼…一路上，也算是有好有坏吧。
t_en: We traveled to many places after we parted ways: Belobog, the Xianzhou Luofu, Penacony... There were both good and bad moments along the way.

t_ch: 丹恒现在怎么样？
t_en: How's Dan Heng doing?

t_ch: 丹恒？虽然中间有不少波折，但他还是回到了罗浮，毕竟，那里本来就是他的故乡。
t_en: Dan Heng? It wasn't an easy journey, but he's back on the Luofu now. After all, that's where he's from.

t_ch: 杨叔现在怎么样？
t_en: How's Mr. Yang?

t_ch: 你知道地球吗？在丹恒离开后不久，瓦尔特如愿以偿找到了回家的路，但愿他能平安抵达那里。
t_en: Have you ever heard of the planet called Earth? Soon after Dan Heng left us, Welt found the way back home just as he had hoped. Let's pray he makes it there safely.

t_ch: 帕姆现在怎么样？
t_en: How's Pom-Pom?

t_ch: 姬子皱了皱眉，似乎你的问题让她感到意外。一阵违和感掠过心头，你从未登上列车，那么你提到的帕姆…是谁？
t_en: Himeko frowns, as if your question has caught her off guard. An unsettling sensation flickers through your mind. You've never boarded the Express. So who is this... "Pom-Pom" you speak of?

t_ch: …你是从哪儿打听到了列车长的名字？是丹恒告诉你的吗？
t_en: ...Where did you hear the conductor's name? Did Dan Heng tell you?

t_ch: 帕姆还是老样子，但看着列车上的人越来越少，总归会有些寂寞吧。
t_en: Pom-Pom's still the same. But with fewer and fewer people on the Express, it must be getting lonely for them.

t_ch: 我有时也会想，如果你跟我们一起离开，会发生什么变化。可惜，开拓之旅从来不存在「后悔」和「如果」。
t_en: I sometimes wonder what might have changed if you had come with us. Sadly, the trailblazing expedition doesn't leave room for regrets and what-ifs.

t_ch: 那三月七呢？
t_en: What about March 7th?

t_ch: 小三月…我不知道该怎么…哎，希望忆庭的朋友能治好她。
t_en: March... I'm not sure how to put it... *heavy sigh* I hope that friend from the Garden can cure her.

t_ch: 有新人上车吗？
t_en: Any new passengers aboard?

t_ch: 列车还缺人吗？
t_en: Is the Express still in need of passengers?

t_ch: 无名客的生活并不轻松，在离开这里后，有些乘客曾和我们短暂同行，但如今都各自散去了。
t_en: Life as a Nameless isn't easy. After we left the space station, we did have some passengers traveling with us for a short time, but they've all gone their separate ways now.

t_ch: 最近，星际和平公司的朋友建议我们去卡美洛一趟，那里应该早就被反物质军团摧毁了，但最近却传出了复数的求救信号。
t_en: Our friends from the Interastral Peace Corporation recently suggested we make a stop at Camelot. That planet should've been destroyed by the Antimatter Legion long ago, but we've been picking up multiple distress signals from there.

t_ch: 我们顺道在空间站停靠一段时间，也来见见一些老朋友——{NICKNAME}，如果你愿意，这回也可以和我们一起离开。
t_en: So we're stopping by the space station for a while to meet some old friends. You can join us and leave with us this time if you want, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 我马上收拾行李！
t_en: I'll pack my bags right away!

t_ch: 你真的是这样想的吗？如果登上星穹列车没能让你的生活变得更好……你该怎么办？
t_en: Is that really how you feel? If joining the Astral Express doesn't change your life for the better... What then?

t_ch: 如果迎接你的不是奇妙的冒险，而是无休止的清洁和修理，是不能愈合的创伤和绝望的死亡……你该怎么办？
t_en: What if what awaits you isn't a grand adventure, but endless cleaning, constant repairs, wounds that never heal, and death steeped in despair... What will you do then?

t_ch: 如果你没能给他们带来什么帮助，如果你成了列车的累赘，如果有一位无名客为了救你而牺牲，如果所有人都希望你离开……你该怎么办？
t_en: What if you can't help anyone? What if you become a dead weight on the Express? What if a Nameless gives their life to save you, yet everyone silently wishes you'd just leave... What will you do?

t_ch: 好好想想，你应该给出怎样的回答？
t_en: Think about it. What answer should you give?

t_ch: 我考虑一下…在列车离开前，我一定会给你答复的。
t_en: I'll think about it... I'll make sure to give you my answer before the Express leaves.

t_ch: 好啊，无论你想要留在哪里，我都会尊重你的选择。
t_en: Alright. I'll respect your choice no matter what you pick.

t_ch: 恬静的梦乡，因窗外的鸟鸣而动摇，可是，真空中也会有知更鸟在唱歌吗？
t_en: A peaceful dream, stirred by birdsong beyond the window. However, can a robin really sing in the vacuum of space?

t_ch: 让我们来说说一只鸟儿的故事吧，一只蒙冤入狱的鸟儿。她曾是一位歌手，因为谱写不受权贵们喜爱的歌谣而被投入铁牢。
t_en: Let me tell you the story of a bird who was imprisoned wrongfully. She was once a singer, thrown into a dark cell for composing songs that the powerful didn't like.

t_ch: 在囚笼中，鸟儿听到了许多悲叹的声音。于是，她为同在笼中的人们歌唱，抚慰他们的思乡愁绪，鼓舞他们起义的壮志。
t_en: The bird heard many voices of sorrow. So, she sang for the others trapped inside, soothing their homesick hearts and inspiring them to rise up.

t_ch: 最终，鸟儿和一块怀表、一头狼还有无数同伴一起冲破了牢笼。
t_en: In the end, the bird broke free, with a pocket watch, a wolf, and countless companions by her side.

t_ch: 鸟儿以为，赢得自由后她能开始全新的生活。她能在舞台上和银幕中展示自己美丽的羽毛与歌喉，像过去那样为一无所有的人们带去希望。
t_en: The bird believed that she would begin a new life after gaining freedom. She would shine on the stage and the silver screen, displaying her beautiful feathers and voice, just as she had once brought hope to the hopeless.

t_ch: 但…她错了。匹诺康尼很快变得面目全非。鸟儿所唱的歌曲，出演的电影，还有她自己的生活，全被关进了名为金钱的笼子里，成了商品。
t_en: But... she was wrong. Penacony soon became unrecognizable. The songs she sang, the films she starred in, and even her own life were all locked away in a cage called money, turned into mere commodities.

t_ch: 电影公司想将鸟儿打造成美丽的符号，鸟儿却想要飞离这片令它窒息的臭水沟……
t_en: The movie studio wanted to turn the bird into a symbol of beauty, but the bird longed to escape from this suffocating, filthy gutter...

t_ch: 于是他们夺走了它的艺名，找来一只全新的雏鸟替代银幕上的她，又用污浊的流言和臆想将她扼死在不名誉的结局里。
t_en: They stripped away her stage name, replaced her with a fresh-faced chick, and smothered her with slander and rumors, leading her to a disgraceful end.

t_ch: …所以，你想报复那些令你蒙受污名的人？
t_en: ...So, you seek revenge on those who tarnished your name?

t_ch: 恰恰相反…再次回到这个世界时，我发现自己已经没有可以报复的对象了。
t_en: No. The exact opposite, in fact. When I returned to this world, I realized there was no one left to take revenge on.

t_ch: 所以我要索回我被夺走的名字，抹消所有属于我的作品……让身为███时存在过的一切痕迹不复存在。
t_en: Instead, I will reclaim the name they took from me and erase every single work I've ever produced... I'll destroy every trace of my existence as ████.

t_ch: 既然曾经被囚禁，被夺取…那么如今您更不应该将所有为终结这场圣杯战争而来的御主与英灵困锁在宝具中。
t_en: You know how it feels to be locked in a cage and have everything taken from you, so why do you still trap the Masters and Heroic Spirits in your Noble Phantasm? When they wish to end this Holy Grail War?

t_ch: 老奥帝先生或许能实现你的愿望，但他索要的却是更高昂的利益。这一点，和他曾同处于一个时代的你比我更清楚。
t_en: Old Oti may be able to fulfill your wish, but the cost he demands is far greater. As someone born in the same era as him, you should know this better than I do.

t_ch: 奥帝是只啃噬匹诺康尼的虫子，他当然不可信。但御主，他是只对圣杯有所求的虫子…而你，圣杯无法收买你的心智。
t_en: Oti is nothing more than a parasite feeding on Penacony... Of course I won't trust him. But you're different, Master. He's a worm who has a wish for the Holy Grail, yet there's nothing the Holy Grail can do to sway you.

t_ch: 想让我解除宝具？那就用令咒命令我吧！御主，像那些人用合同与法律夺走我的名字、事业一样，来吧！
t_en: If you want to stop my Noble Phantasm, you'll have to force me with a Command Spell! Come, Master! Take away my free will, just like how they took away my name and career with their contracts and laws!

t_ch: 你知道我不会这么做的，音符小姐！…我们来打个赌吧，虽然我这辈子从未和别人赌过什么。
t_en: You know I won't do that, Miss Note! ...Let's make a wager, though I've never once gambled in my life.

t_ch: 我打赌那些被你剥夺「英雄」妆容的御主与从者，绝不会被你的宝具困住——我对他们有信心！
t_en: I bet that those Masters and Servants will not yield to your Noble Phantasm, even though you've stripped their identity as heroes — I trust them.

t_ch: ……那么，我将拭目以待。
t_en: ...I will wait and see.

t_ch: 鸣鸟的歌声沉默了。
t_en: The birdsong fades into silence.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，这是你在空间站工作的第几天？第三十天？第九十天？还是更久？
t_en: {NICKNAME}, how many days has it been since you started working at the space station? Thirty? Ninety? Even longer?

t_ch: 还记得你现在要做什么吗？收集各级科员的课题申请，对现在的你来说，这简直是闭上眼都能完成的工作。
t_en: Do you still remember what you need to do now? Compiling project proposals from all levels of researchers. By now, it's second nature — something you could do with your eyes closed.

t_ch: 轻松完成工作。
t_en: Breeze through the work.

t_ch: 轻松完成工作。
t_en: Breeze through the work.

t_ch: 轻松完成工作。
t_en: Breeze through the work.

t_ch: 轻松完成工作。
t_en: Breeze through the work.

t_ch: 轻松完成工作。
t_en: Breeze through the work.

t_ch: 轻松完成工作。
t_en: Breeze through the work.

t_ch: 还在想星穹列车的事吗？你已经为这事纠结了很久，但仍没能做出决定。
t_en: Still thinking about the Astral Express? You've been wrestling with that choice for ages, and still haven't made up your mind.

t_ch: 他们很快就要启程了，你祝愿他们能邂逅美好的旅程。
t_en: They'll be departing soon. You wish them a journey full of wondrous encounters.

t_ch: 我想留下来。
t_en: I want to stay.

t_ch: 我想离开。
t_en: I want to leave.

t_ch: 我不知道该去哪儿。
t_en: I don't know where to go.

t_ch: 如果登上列车，等待你的，只会是可怕的未知和危险。你的功绩终会被遗忘，你的旅程也终会迎来终点。
t_en: If you board the Express, what waits for you may be nothing but danger and the terrifying unknown. Your deeds will be forgotten in time, and your journey will eventually come to an end.

t_ch: 我很高兴留在这里。
t_en: I'm glad I stayed.

t_ch: 我渴望平凡的生活。
t_en: I long for a quiet, ordinary life.

t_ch: 让我离开！
t_en: Let me go!

t_ch: 所以，你很高兴自己留在了这里，没有车票，没有球棒，没有危险，平静而安逸的生活。
t_en: So you are glad you stayed. No ticket. No bat. No danger. Just a quiet, peaceful, and undisturbed life.

t_ch: 手头的工作耽误了你太多时间，<color=#dbc291ff>一天，两天，十天……</color>
t_en: The work you had piled up took far too much of your time, <color=#dbc291ff>one day, two days, ten days...</color>

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>或许你再也追不上星穹列车了</color>。
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>Maybe you'll never catch up to the Astral Express again</color>.

t_ch: 这不是我们的星核{F#小姐}{M#小哥}嘛，原本打算给你调一杯酒的，看在你未成年的份上，还是算了。
t_en: Isn't that our Stellaron {M#boy?}{F#girl?} I was about to mix you a drink, but since you're still underage, I'll hold off for now.

t_ch: 我觉得现在的生活是假的。
t_en: I feel like my current life is a lie.

t_ch: 我曾经是列车的开拓者。
t_en: I was once a Trailblazer on the Express.

t_ch: 我不应该是地概科科员。
t_en: I shouldn't be a member of the Department of Cosmic Geography.

t_ch: 巧了不是？我有时也觉得，自己不该是个空间站保安。
t_en: Funny, isn't it? Sometimes I think I shouldn't be working as a space station security guard either.

t_ch: 所以，你想登上星穹列车？
t_en: So, do you want to board the Astral Express?

t_ch: 没错。
t_en: That's right.

t_ch: 像你这种想要登上列车的科员，每年都会出来几个，可事到临头，他们毫无例外都退缩了。
t_en: Every year there are researchers who want to go with the Express, but when it's time to leave, they all back down without fail.

t_ch: 他们明白，自己只是想要逃避沉重的生活，而非一场随时会交代掉小命的冒险。
t_en: They know they are just seeking an escape from their stressful lives, instead of actually wanting an adventure that could cost them their life.

t_ch: 所以，你是哪一种？
t_en: So, what do you really want?

t_ch: 我想要离开现在的生活。
t_en: I want to leave my current life behind.

t_ch: 我向往一场未知的冒险。
t_en: I want to go on an adventure into the unknown.

t_ch: 「保持现状就好」、「反正会有更适合的人」、「我只是微不足道的凡人」。
t_en: "Staying where I am is fine." "Someone better will come along." "I'm just an ordinary person."

t_ch: 后悔？这世上哪有不会后悔的人？
t_en: Why speak of regrets when everyone who has ever lived has regretted something?

t_ch: 可如果人人都因它们而踌躇不前，我想，世上就不会有英雄了。
t_en: Yet, had everyone turned back because of their fear of regrets, humanity would never have produced any heroes.

t_ch: 即使不知道前路如何，仍旧有人出发了，为了追逐某个愿望。这固然愚蠢，但正是这种愚蠢，催生了无名客、开拓者，催生了英雄。
t_en: Even if the road ahead is unclear, some still set off, driven by the desire to chase a wish. It may seem foolish, but that's what gave birth to the Nameless, to Trailblazers, and to humanity's heroes.

t_ch: 你身上的那个玩偶，就是某位英雄存在过的证明。
t_en: The doll you carry is proof of a certain hero's existence.

t_ch: 你知道这是什么吗？
t_en: Do you know what it is?

t_ch: 一个蹩脚的工艺品罢了。却因为某人天真的愿望，最终在历史中留下的{RUBY_B#星星}记录{RUBY_E#}。
t_en: A shabby little trinket that left a {RUBY_B#star}trace{RUBY_E#} in history because of one person's silly wish.

t_ch: 列车或许已经出发了，又或许没有，但只要你真的想要踏上这条道路，即使没有列车又如何？
t_en: The Express may have already left, or it may not have, but if you truly want to walk this path, what does it matter if there's no Express?

t_ch: 如果你真觉得自己是无名客，那你要做的只有一件事情。
t_en: If you truly believe you are a Nameless, then there is only one thing left for you to do.

t_ch: 我要出发了。
t_en: I must set off.

t_ch: 我要出发了。
t_en: I must set off.

t_ch: 我要出发了。
t_en: I must set off.

t_ch: 我该出发了！
t_en: I need to set off!

t_ch: 不要再前进了，无名客的未来只有一片虚无和绝望。
t_en: Stop. The future of a Nameless is nothing but emptiness and despair.

t_ch: 你以为自己很特别吗？那些比你更优秀的无名客…铁尔南也好，凯勒贝克也罢，他们全都被黑暗的深空吞没，再也没有回来！
t_en: Do you think you're special? Think of Tiernan and Kelbeck... The Nameless who were better than you... they were all consumed by the lightless outer space, never to return!

t_ch: 所以你终于承认…
t_en: So you'll finally admit...

t_ch: 我是个无名客了吗？
t_en: That I'm a Nameless?

t_ch: 所以，你终于承认，我是一个无名客了？
t_en: So you'll finally admit... that I'm a Nameless?

t_ch: 所有向你说话的声音在瞬间沉默了，他们不再开口。
t_en: The voices that once spoke to you fell silent in an instant. They never spoke again.

t_ch: 球棒，平平无奇，却如此熟悉。
t_en: A bat — plain, unimpressive, yet strangely familiar.

t_ch: 你一度以为自己此生再也不会有握起它的机会。
t_en: You once believed you'd never hold it again in this lifetime.

t_ch: 即便拔起它意味着不幸的结局…你们这些人也会照做不误吗？
t_en: Would you still pull this weapon out, even if it'll bring about a tragic end?

t_ch: 梅林也曾这么警告过我。
t_en: That's what Merlin told me, too.

t_ch: 所以，我为你们编织了一个个温柔平和的梦境。这不正是你们内心渴求的东西吗？
t_en: Which is why I've crafted such gentle dreams for you all. Isn't that what you've been looking for all this time?

t_ch: 我确实想过，如果有另一个人拔出了选王之剑，也许他会比我做得更好。
t_en: I did wonder whether someone else might have done a better job as king if they pulled out the Sword of Selection.

t_ch: 但对于那个曾经拔起石中剑的女孩来说，我无法责难她不自量力的心。她只是想像那位挑战白龙的王一样，试图去拯救些什么。
t_en: But I can't blame the girl who drew the Sword in the Stone for overestimating herself. She just wanted to deliver something from disaster... just like the king who challenged the White Dragon.

t_ch: 如果还有下一次选择的机会，我会告诉她：希望这一回你能比过去的自己，做得更好一些。
t_en: If I was given another chance to make my choice, I would tell her, "I hope you can do better this time."

t_ch: 御主，在平和幸福的梦境中想必已经休息够了吧？现在，让我们一起离开这里了！
t_en: Master, you've had enough rest in your happy dream, haven't you? Now, let's leave this place together!

t_ch: 你们一定会后悔的。
t_en: You'll regret this.

t_ch: 握住球棒。
t_en: Grab the bat.

t_ch: 从不。
t_en: I'll never regret.

t_ch: （这报表怎么好像无穷无尽的…而且，它们记录的东西也很奇怪。）
t_en: (These reports are endless... and the stuff written in there is really weird.)

t_ch: （等等，那边的人，总感觉很熟悉。）
t_en: (Hold on. I think I've seen that person over there before.)

t_ch: （好累，感觉已经没法思考了。）
t_en: (I'm exhausted... I feel like I can't even think anymore.)

t_ch: （可我还是觉得，自己不应该属于这里。）
t_en: (But deep down? I still feel like I don't belong here.)

t_ch: （好古怪的梦…该去做今天的工作了。）
t_en: (What a strange dream... I should get to work.)

t_ch: （预约未来一个月的地概科会议，应该比昨天轻松一些吧。）
t_en: (Scheduling Department of Cosmic Geography meetings for the next month... this should be easier than yesterday, right?)

t_ch: （星穹列车，我拒绝了他们的邀请……）
t_en: (The Astral Express... I turned down their invitation back then...)

t_ch: （我那时的选择，真的是正确的吗？）
t_en: (Did I really make the right choice?)

t_ch: （好累，还是先回去吧，星穹列车……）
t_en: (I'm so tired... maybe I should head back first. The Astral Express...)

t_ch: （似乎做了个奇怪的梦，但细节怎么也想不起来……）
t_en: (I feel like I had a strange dream, but the details keep slipping away...)

t_ch: （身体好沉，不行，还有今天的工作要做。）
t_en: (My body feels so heavy... But I can't rest. There's still today's work to get through.)

t_ch: 你累了，找个房间休息一会儿吧。
t_en: You're tired. Find a room and take a break.

t_ch: 列车已经离开了，过去只会扑一场空。
t_en: The Express's already left. Going there will be pointless.

t_ch: 我们哪儿有拦着你？{NICKNAME}，有空聚个餐吗？
t_en: We're not holding you back, {NICKNAME}. Let's have lunch together!

t_ch: 到研究室去！到餐厅去！去钓鱼，去看电影吧！
t_en: Let's go to the lab! Hit the cafeteria! Let's go fishing together! Let's go watch a movie!

t_ch: （有什么在阻止我离开这里，我得躲开他们。）
t_en: (Something's stopping me from leaving this place. I need to avoid them.)

t_ch: 在空间站的生活不好吗？有多少人会羡慕你在这里的生活？
t_en: Isn't life on the space station wonderful? People would kill just to live the life you have here!

t_ch: 这里没有反物质军团，没有丰饶孽物，这里是宇宙最安稳的乐土。
t_en: There's no Antimatter Legion, no Abominations of Abundance... This place is the most peaceful paradise in the universe.

t_ch: 为什么…你们会有如此盲目的勇气？
t_en: Why... do you have such blind courage?

t_ch: 难道你们从不会感到后悔吗？
t_en: Don't you ever feel regret?

t_ch: 我…无法成为像你们一样的愚者…
t_en: I... can't become a fool like you...

t_ch: 我也不会认同你们的选择！
t_en: And I won't agree with your choices!

t_ch: 和活着时一样，我失败了。身为背叛者，这倒是个…恰如其分的结局。
t_en: I failed, just like how I failed in life. This is... a fitting end for a traitor.

t_ch: 音符小姐，既然舞台曾让你如此痛苦，为什么在离开囚牢后，你又一次选择留在了匹诺康尼的舞台上？
t_en: The performing arts had given you so much pain, Miss Note... Why did you choose to remain on Penacony's stage after leaving your prison?

t_ch: 我还记得每当我唱歌时，粗鲁的囚犯就会安静下来，哭闹的孩子们停止啜泣开始微笑。因为这些微不足道的事情，即便在铁牢里，我仍然感觉自己是自由的。
t_en: Because... I still remember how the rough prisoners fell silent, and the crying children smiled whenever I started to sing. Those small moments made me feel free even in that iron cage.

t_ch: 但匹诺康尼变了，这么多琥珀纪过去后，它变得更陌生了。而我被这个新生的牢笼锁住了。
t_en: But Penacony has changed. After so many Amber Eras, it's become almost unrecognizable. And now, I am locked in this newly-forged prison.

t_ch: 够了，现在是时候结束这一切了，背叛御主的从者没有存在的资格！
t_en: Anyway, it's time to end this. A Servant who betrayed their Master has no right to exist!

t_ch: 既然如此，我会如你所愿……
t_en: Then, as you wish...

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>我以第一划令咒下令，阿斯娜，我代表匹诺康尼归还你的名字。</color>
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>With the first Command Spell, I return your name to you on behalf of Penacony, Asna.</color>

t_ch: 即使他们将你的名字注册成了商标，夺走了你的一切，你也绝非一文不名的失败者。
t_en: Even if they have trademarked your name and stripped you of everything, that does not make you an unknown failure.

t_ch: 抬起头来，鸢尾花之母！<color=#dbc291ff>我以第二划令咒下令，归还你身为英灵的证明。</color>
t_en: Hold your head high, Mother of Iris! <color=#dbc291ff>With the second Command Spell, I return to you the testament of your existence as a Heroic Spirit.</color>

t_ch: 因为你的逝去，歌者和艺人们开始懂得为自己从商人手中争取自由和尊严。你绝不是什么寂寂无名的影子，你是匹诺康尼五大家系中鸢尾花一支的创始者！
t_en: Your death was what made singers and artists realize the need to reclaim their freedom and dignity from merchants. You are not some forgotten shadow, you are the founder of the Iris family, one of the five major lineages of Penacony!

t_ch: 正因如此，你才会借用那个名字，不是吗？由你的学生和朋友，梅芙恩和钟表匠共同创造的形象——在美梦小镇中自在歌唱的「音符小姐」。
t_en: That's why you chose to borrow that name, isn't it? A figure shaped by your students and friends, by Maeven and the Watchmaker — "Miss Note," the one who sang freely in Dreamville.

t_ch: 请不要再说下去了……
t_en: Please no more... I don't want to hear anymore...

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>我以第三划令咒下令，作为舞台上的后辈，我归还您身为人的自由。</color>
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>With the third Command Spell, as your junior on the stage, I return to you your freedom as a person.</color>

t_ch: 从今往后，鸢尾花之母，您不再受任何人的摆布，只为自己起舞。
t_en: Mother of Iris, you will no longer be controlled by anyone from this moment on. You will now only dance for yourself.

t_ch: 这场闹剧就要结束了，但在那之前——我希望您能走出大剧院，亲眼看看那些艺者，无论曾有多少诋毁，在他们眼中，您是一位「英雄」。
t_en: This farce is nearing its end, but I hope you can step out of the Grand Theater before you disappear, and meet Penacony's artists. You are still a "hero" to them — No matter the slanders your name bears.

t_ch: 与此同时，脱离梦境的御主与从者们走进了大剧院的中心……
t_en: Meanwhile, the Master and their Servants, freed from the dreamscape, stepped into the heart of the Grand Theater...

t_ch: 我感到了一股类似魔力的强大力量，看来，圣杯的持有者已接近完成仪式了。
t_en: I sense a powerful force similar to magical energy... It seems the Holy Grail's holder is about to complete the ritual.

t_ch: 余下的两骑，Rider和Berserker，迎接我们的会是谁？还是二者皆有？在对方已占据圣杯的情况下，看来会是一场恶战。
t_en: The last two Servants left are Rider and Berserker. Which one will face us? Or will it be both? With the enemy already in possession of the Holy Grail, it looks like we're in for a brutal fight.

t_ch: 三打二，优势在我。
t_en: Three against two. The advantage is mine.

t_ch: 下次还填非常简单。
t_en: I'll once again select "Too Easy" in the next survey.

t_ch: 我将带头冲锋。
t_en: I'll lead the charge.

t_ch: 御主，我向这把剑起誓，会保护你的安全。
t_en: Master, I swear on this sword that I will protect you, no matter the cost.

t_ch: 「另一种人生」…匹诺康尼的梦泡确实有害健康。那场谐乐大典要走了我半条命……刚才那个从者所编织的幻境又拿走了剩下一半。
t_en: "A different life"...? Penacony's dream bubbles are pure cancer. The Charmony Festival nearly killed me... and that Servant's fantasy almost finished the job.

t_ch: 所以，你被她关到哪儿去了？
t_en: So, where did she put you?

t_ch: 一个不管赌上什么也永远赢不了的梦…在那里我成了一个老老实实的侍者，戒掉了所有押注的念头。
t_en: I had a dream where I could never win... I got to live as an obedient serf and gave up on even the thought of gambling.

t_ch: 他宝贝的，戒赌…还真是富有教育意义。
t_en: Darn right. Quit gambling... that's quite the educational dream you had there.

t_ch: 你呢？你又被送去了哪里？
t_en: And you? Where did she send you?

t_ch: 我嘛…只是经历了一次久违的重逢罢了。
t_en: Me? I had... a long-awaited reunion.

t_ch: 呦，这不是第一个被关进固有结界的英灵吗？没好好活用你那两条骄傲的腿吗？
t_en: Yo. If it isn't the first Heroic Spirit to get trapped in her Reality Marble. Didn't you use those long legs of yours well enough?

t_ch: 刚一回来就要听你说这些屁话。诶呀我去，头好疼啊！简直像经历了一场漫长又惊心动魄的冒险！
t_en: This is the first thing I hear after I got out? Darn it, my head's killing me! Feels like I've been through a long and thrilling adventure!

t_ch: 不知道为什么，记忆里对方似乎长着鱼的脑袋。
t_en: Not sure why, but I think my enemies all had fish heads on their necks.

t_ch: 嚯嗬嗬嗬！看来阿斯娜女士也失败了。可惜，只差一步，她就能将各位从者和梦的火焰剥离下来，作为燃料投入圣杯。
t_en: Ohohoho! Looks like Lady Asna has failed. What a shame. She was just one step away from tearing the flame of dreams off the Servants, and tossing them into the Holy Grail as fuel.

t_ch: 这就是商业合作，不管你的合作者有多么出色，最重要的环节还得自己来解决。
t_en: But that's the business world for you. No matter how capable your collaborator is, you've gotta handle the most critical parts yourself.

t_ch: 所以，你这老可爱搞的这「圣杯战争」，到底是想整什么呜呜伯？
t_en: Old fudgehead. It's time to tell us what kind of stinkin' pie you're cooking up with this "Holy Grail War."
